mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls signal deepening recessionary conditions, reducing inflation expectations and risk appetite, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield downward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change against 20-day yield change is below 0.03 in any 2-year rolling window, or the mean yield change following negative payroll prints is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a severe labor market deterioration that depresses long-term growth expectations, causing a flight-to-safety that pushes down the 10-year yield within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the 20-day forward yield change is positive (increase) in more than 60% of cases, or the average yield change is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of very low federal funds rate and rising consumer price index implies deeply negative real interest rates, which elevates inflation expectations and term premium, driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL month-over-month change) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression on 60-day yield changes, or the signal fails to produce positive mean yield change in the top quintile of interaction strength.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle (FEDFUNDS below 1.0%), declining industrial production signals that rate cuts have not yet revived economic activity, reinforcing deflationary fears and pushing the 10-year yield lower over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the 20-day yield change is positive (increase) in more than 50% of instances, or the mean yield change is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales signals resilient consumer demand, which lifts expectations for near-term economic activity and inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 5-day yield change is not positive, or the mean yield change following positive RSAFS surprises is not significantly above zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, it marks the effective lower bound and signals that the central bank has exhausted conventional easing, which triggers market speculation about additional unconventional measures that initially push yields lower on a flight-to-quality within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5%, the 20-day yield change is positive (increase) in more than 50% of cases, or the average yield change is not negative at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low federal funds rate and rising housing starts indicates that monetary accommodation is successfully stimulating interest-rate-sensitive demand, signaling an impending economic recovery that lifts inflation expectations and the 10-year yield over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal (low FEDFUNDS and positive HOUST surprises) does not produce a positive mean 60-day yield change in the top decile of interaction strength, or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) falls below the pre-recession level of 24,000 billion dollars, further declines in output reinforce recession expectations and deflationary pressures, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 is below 24,000 and falls further, the 60-day yield change is positive (increase) in more than 60% of instances, or the rank correlation between GDPC1 level and subsequent yield change is not negative.
